reasoning: Well-constructed CBC padding oracle with genuine depth: custom XOR-0x42 padding scheme defeats off-the-shelf tools, timing-based oracle (50ms sleep) rather than distinct error codes adds real subtlety, and PoW gates rate-limit brute-force queries. Notable discrepancy between the intended solution description (claims distinct ERR_DSAS_001/ERR_DSAS_002 codes) and the actual implementation (returns identical 0x80041001 for all failures, oracle is purely timing-based), which is a documentation flaw but ironically makes the challenge harder. Build errors in generation logs and missing flag-submission endpoint are minor concerns offset by excellent flag isolation (encrypted at build, source removed).
